meaning: vi.乱窜,猛冲,(车等)颠簸,沸腾作用vt.猛击,击溃n.重击,冲击力,乐趣
examples: With one brutal wallop, Leticia flattened him.
The precise health wallop you suffer may have to do with how your body manifests stress.
They hang them upside down and use heavy batons and koboko to wallop them all over their body, and tell them to confess.
This wallop would come on top of any gas taxes we already pay to our states.
